task_completion.description: Verify that 3 tags associated with the 'Packer' equipment have been created in Crimson 3.0.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Navigation Pane under 'Data Tags' in the Crimson 3.0 interface. There should be exactly 3 tags configured that correspond to the Packer.

task_completion.description: Ensure no tags assigned to the 'Palletizer' are configured.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Examine the 'Data Tags' list in Crimson 3.0. There should be no tags named or associated with the Palletizer.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'Multi-State' formatting is applied to the Packer tags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the tag properties in the main workspace, specifically under the 'Format' tab. The 'Format Type' dropdown or selection must be set to 'Multi-State'.

task_completion.description: Verify that raw PLC integer values are mapped to the correct human-readable strings with exact casing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Examine the Multi-State configuration area in the Format tab. You should see integer values (e.g., 0, 1, 2) mapped to specific text strings representing machine states (e.g., 'Stopped', 'Starting', 'Idle'). The casing must be exact.

task_completion.description: Verify the project is saved with the correct filename in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Crimson 3.0 application title bar for 'packaging_packml' or a File Explorer window showing the file 'packaging_packml.c3' located in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Red Lion Crimson 3.0 GUI to configure the tags.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Crimson 3.0 application open with active configuration of tags, rather than just a script or terminal window.

integrity.description: Verify the agent opened the provided reference files to obtain the mappings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show 'packml_tag_register.csv' and/or 'omac_state_model.txt' opened in a text editor, spreadsheet viewer, or terminal to read the required tag and state data.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the GUI by using unauthorized terminal commands to generate the proprietary .c3 file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal windows showing commands that directly write or copy a pre-made .c3 file into the target directory without GUI interaction.
